title: CASE-compatible exporter (1EdTech CASE v1.1) + golden fixtures
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. CASE is the standards-interop format consumers ingest; this exporter must validate against the pinned CASE v1.1 spec.
objective: Build the CASE v1.1 exporter with committed golden fixtures validated against the pinned CASE schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Emits valid CASE v1.1 JSON for framework/standard references (version in specVersions).
Ships committed golden canonical-record -> expected-CASE pairs, diffed in CI and validated against the pinned CASE schema.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
